title: Dell NextGen Presales Academy 2026 in Amsterdam: Launch Your Tech Career with Dell Technologies
summary: Applications are now open for the NextGen Presales Academy at Dell Technologies , a unique training program designed to help recent graduates start their careers in the technology industry. The program is based in Amsterdam , Netherlands, and offers participants the opportunity to develop the skills needed to become Inside Presales Solution Architects. Through structured training and hands-on learning, the program prepares graduates to work as trusted technical advisors in one of the world s leading technology companies. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the Dell NextGen Presales Academy The NextGen Presales Academy is an intensive professional development program that runs for 12 to 24 months. It is designed to transform talented graduates into highly skilled presales professionals who can bridge the gap between technology solutions and customer needs. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Participants will receive training on Dell s technology solutions, customer engagement strategies, and technical consulting practices. The program also emphasizes developing communication, analytical, and problem-solving skills that are essential for working with clients in complex IT environments. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); What Participants Will Learn During the academy, participants will gain practical knowledge and industry experience through structured training modules and real-world projects. The program focuses on developing expertise in: Technology consulting and presales strategies Understanding enterprise IT infrastructure and solutions Customer engagement and relationship management Technical presentation and communication skills Problem-solving and analytical thinking in technology environments ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); By the end of the program, graduates will be equipped with the knowledge and confidence to support customers in selecting and implementing technology solutions. Who Should Apply The NextGen Presales Academy is designed for recent graduates who have a passion for technology and want to build a career in technical consulting or solution architecture. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Ideal candidates are individuals who are curious about emerging technology trends and enjoy solving complex problems while working closely with customers and business teams. Requirements Applicants should meet the following criteria: A degree in a technical or business-related field Strong interest in IT and technology trends Excellent communication and relationship-building skills An analytical mindset with a strong drive to achieve results ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Candidates who demonstrate curiosity, adaptability, and a willingness to learn will thrive in this program. Why This Program Matters Programs like the NextGen Presales Academy provide an important pathway for young professionals to enter the technology industry. Rather than requiring years of experience, the program focuses on developing talent through training, mentorship, and hands-on practice. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Participants gain exposure to real-world business challenges while working with experienced professionals, helping them build a strong foundation for long-term careers in technology consulting and enterprise solutions. How to Apply Interested candidates can learn more and submit their applications through the official application portal. For more job opportunities from OFY, Click here. title: ITCILO Paid Internships 2026 in Italy Open to Applicants Worldwide | International Training Centre of the ILO Internship Programme
summary: Students and recent graduates interested in international development, labour policy, employment, and social protection can now apply for the ITCILO Paid Internship Programme 2026 at the International Training Centre of the International Labour Organization (ITCILO) in Turin, Italy. This programme offers young professionals the opportunity to gain practical experience within a United Nations agency while contributing to global labour and development initiatives aligned with the mandate of the International Labour Organization (ILO). ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the ITCILO Internship Programme The ITCILO Internship Programme provides graduate students, postgraduate students, recent graduates, and representatives of labour institutions with the opportunity to gain practical experience in an international environment. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Interns work alongside experts in areas related to the ILO s mandate and the Centre s activities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The main objectives of the internship programme are to: Increase understanding of the ILO s objectives, policies, standards, and programmes Provide practical work experience related to interns academic specialization Offer exposure to international development and labour policy work Allow participants to work in a multicultural and international professional environment The International Training Centre of the ILO has approximately 170 permanent staff members from more than 35 countries , creating a diverse and collaborative working environment. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Internship Location Location: ITCILO Campus, Turin, Italy The International Training Centre of the ILO is located in Turin, one of Italy s key cities for international training, policy dialogue, and development cooperation. Why Apply for the ITCILO Internship? The internship programme offers a valuable opportunity for students and early-career professionals interested in global development and labour policy. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Benefits include: Hands-on experience in a United Nations agency Work in a multicultural and international environment Contribute to global development and social justice initiatives Gain exposure to international labour standards and employment policies Build professional networks with experts in international development Available ITCILO Internship Opportunities 2026 Multiple internship positions are currently available across different programmes at the Centre. Learning Innovation Programme (LIP) Application deadline: 10 April 2026 Focuses on digital learning tools, training innovation, and global capacity-building programmes. Development and Investment Programme (DEVINVEST) Application deadline: 10 April 2026 Supports work related to development finance, sustainable investment, and economic development initiatives. Programme for Employers Activities (ACTEMP) Application deadline: 10 April 2026 Works with employers organizations to strengthen labour markets, enterprise development, and business environments. Programme for Workers Activities (ACTRAV) Application deadline: 10 April 2026 Focuses on workers rights, trade unions, labour standards, and social dialogue. Organizational Development and Project Services Programme (ODPS) Application deadline: 10 April 2026 Supports programme management, project implementation, and organizational development. Sustainable Enterprises and Economies Programme (SEE) Application deadline: 10 April 2026 Works on sustainable enterprise development, green jobs, and inclusive economic growth. Human Resources Services Medical Service (HRS) Application deadline: 10 April 2026 Supports occupational health and employee wellbeing services within the Centre. International Labour Standards, Rights at Work and Gender Equality Programme (ILSGEN) Application deadline: 10 April 2026 Focuses on labour rights, gender equality, and international labour standards. Information and Communication Technology Services (ICTS) Disability Reserved Application deadline: 10 May 2026 Dedicated internship opportunity for candidates with disabilities focusing on ICT services and digital systems. Social Protection Governance and Tripartism Programme (SPGT) Application deadline: 10 April 2026 Supports initiatives related to social protection systems and tripartite governance involving governments, employers, and workers. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Office of the Director (DIR) Application deadline: 10 April 2026 Provides exposure to executive leadership, programme coordination, and institutional strategy. Apply here: https://www.itcilo.org/about/jobs Who Can Apply The internship programme is open to: Graduate students Postgraduate students Recent graduates Representatives of employers organizations Representatives of workers organizations Representatives of labour ministries Applicants should be pursuing or have completed studies in fields such as: International development Labour and employment policy Economics Social protection Public policy Human rights Gender studies Information technology Organizational development ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Important Application Information All candidates must complete an online application form . Applications submitted by email will not be considered . For additional inquiries, applicants may contact: internships@itcilo.org How to Apply Interested candidates should apply through the official ITCILO recruitment portal. Apply here: https://www.itcilo.org/about/jobs Final Thoughts The ITCILO Paid Internship Programme 2026 provides a valuable opportunity for students and young professionals seeking careers in international development, labour policy, and global governance . Interns gain exposure to international labour standards, employment policies, and development programmes while working in a multicultural professional environment at the International Training Centre of the ILO. Apply now and take the first step towards an impactful international career. title: Earth Journalism Network: Applications Open for Biodiversity Media Grants 2026
summary: The Biodiversity Media Grants 2026, offered by the Earth Journalism Network (EJN), are now inviting applications from eligible media organizations across low- and middle-income countries. As biodiversity loss accelerates at an unprecedented rate, this grant program aims to strengthen the quality and quantity of reporting that can reshape public understanding and support urgent conservation action. About the Earth Journalism Network Founded in 2004, the Earth Journalism Network has grown into one of the world s largest alliances of environmental reporters and media platforms. With misinformation rising and newsroom budgets shrinking, journalists often lack the resources to conduct in-depth investigations into biodiversity, conservation, and climate change. EJN addresses this gap by supporting evidence-driven reporting, capacity building, and global peer learning. The Biodiversity Media Grants form a crucial pillar of this mission. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Why These Grants Matter The latest global biodiversity assessments reveal a troubling reality: trillions are still being invested in activities that harm nature, while only a fraction supports conservation. This imbalance threatens ecosystems and future generations, affecting everything from food security and public health to economies and cultural heritage. Despite the severity of the crisis, biodiversity issues remain underreported and misunderstood. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Recognizing this gap, the Biodiversity Media Initiative funds projects that deepen public knowledge, amplify local voices, and build journalistic expertise. With grants ranging from EUR 10,000 to 12,000, selected organizations will be empowered to produce compelling stories and strengthen the media s ability to cover biodiversity issues with accuracy, nuance, and urgency. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); What the Grant Supports EJN will fund three to four organizations whose project proposals demonstrate strong public value and journalistic rigor. Successful projects may include: Investigative or data-led reporting that unpacks biodiversity threats and highlights solutions. Cross-border collaborations connecting journalists across regions. Training programs, workshops, or field reporting trips aimed at strengthening biodiversity reporting skills. Tools and platforms such as mapping resources, fact-checking systems, and e-learning modules to support environmental storytelling. Networks and coalitions of reporters committed to biodiversity coverage. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Projects must primarily target audiences in low- and middle-income countries, where biodiversity loss is often most severe and where quality reporting can spark community-driven change. Priority Themes for 2026 In this round, EJN is especially interested in proposals focusing on: OECMs (Other Effective Area-Based Conservation Measures), particularly those linked to global 30 30 targets. Business impacts on nature , including supply-chain footprints and dependencies. Collaborative conservation approaches involving communities, governments, or private actors. Nature-based solutions that link biodiversity with health, climate resilience, or food security. Unintended harms of conservation measures where local livelihoods or rights may be affected. Biodiversity health interconnections , such as zoonotic risks or ecosystem-based health benefits. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Eligibility Requirements Eligible applicants include journalist networks, media outlets, civil society organizations, and academic institutions based in low- or middle-income countries. EJN particularly encourages applications from biodiversity-rich regions or places experiencing rapid ecological decline. To be considered, applicants must: Propose projects with significant biodiversity impact. Submit applications in English . Demonstrate journalistic expertise or strong partnerships with media professionals. Avoid advocacy-only or politically motivated proposals. Disclose any use of generative AI tools in preparing the application. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Previous EJN grantees may apply, although recent grantees from 2024 will not be funded again. Application Deadline All applications must be submitted by 28 March 2026 . Late or incomplete submissions will not be considered. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Why You Should Apply At a time when biodiversity is under unprecedented pressure, strong journalism is essential to inform the public, influence policy, and support community-led solutions. This grant empowers media organizations to investigate urgent issues, amplify local knowledge, and shape conversations that matter for future generations. If your organization is committed to high-impact storytelling and wants to contribute to global biodiversity awareness, the Biodiversity Media Grants 2026 offer a valuable opportunity to make a difference. Application can be accessed from here . To know more about such opportunities, click here . The post Earth Journalism Network: Applications Open for Biodiversity Media Grants 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Applications for Urban Studies Foundation (USF) Open: Urban Urgencies Research Grants 2026
summary: The Urban Studies Foundation (USF) has announced a major new funding opportunity for researchers and organisations working at the forefront of urban challenges. Through its Urban Urgencies initiative, the Foundation will award up to GBP 35,000 to support innovative, collaborative primary research addressing the urgent issues shaping cities today. The deadline to apply is 23 March 2026 . About the Urban Studies Foundation Established in Scotland in 2008, the Urban Studies Foundation is dedicated to advancing academic research and education in the field of urban studies worldwide. The organisation provides substantial grant funding to support research that drives new insights, strengthens global scholarship, and mobilises knowledge for policy, governance, and social impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Over the years, the Foundation has supported influential work across continents, making it a key contributor to interdisciplinary urban research. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the Urban Urgencies Grant Urban Urgencies is a new research funding call designed to seed-fund up to six collaborative projects, each receiving up to 35,000 . Projects must begin within nine months of the application deadline and may run for up to 18 months. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); At its core, the scheme aims to generate rigorous, rapid-response research that both documents and shapes responses to pressing urban challenges. These may include climate change, housing insecurity, public health crises, erosion of democratic institutions, conflict and urbicide, technological disruptions such as AI, or other emerging uncertainties. The USF deliberately avoids fixing a narrow theme. Instead, researchers are encouraged to identify urgent issues grounded in specific places, communities, and contexts, while engaging deeply with broader theoretical debates in urban studies. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); What the Foundation Is Looking For Proposals must demonstrate a clear, compelling contribution to the academic field of urban studies and show how new evidence will advance dialogue across theory, policy, and practice. Importantly, projects must involve at least one non-academic partner such as a civil society organisation, public institution, or community-based group. This collaboration is key, as the Foundation emphasises work that bridges knowledge production, capacity strengthening, and real-world impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); The initiative prioritises interdisciplinary and transdisciplinary approaches and strongly encourages early-career researchers to apply. Successful projects should not only generate new research findings but also produce accessible outputs for public audiences such as briefs, reports, or creative knowledge-mobilisation materials. The Importance of Studying Urban Urgencies Today s urban landscapes are shaped by overlapping crises environmental, economic, political, and social. Rising inequality, ecological instability, forced displacement, and rapid technological change have intensified the vulnerabilities of urban populations across both the Global North and Global South. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Against this backdrop, the Urban Urgencies initiative calls for research that is not only analytical but transformative, exploring how cities can move toward equity, justice, and resilience. Projects might examine shifting power dynamics, new forms of urban governance, or emerging solidarities and innovations that challenge traditional urban paradigms. By funding such work, the Urban Studies Foundation hopes to deepen understanding of how cities are evolving and how they might be reshaped for the better. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Expected Outputs and Outcomes Funded projects must produce outputs with global relevance to urban studies. The Foundation welcomes plans for academic publications and encourages applicants to consider contributing to leading journals in the field. Projects should also integrate knowledge-mobilisation strategies that speak to policymakers, community actors, and broader publics. In addition to publications, researchers may produce policy briefs, public reports, interactive tools, or other forms of dissemination that amplify the project s impact. Eligibility and Application The call is open to researchers and organisations worldwide , with support available for modest overheads in ODA-eligible countries. Applicants must form a partnership between at least two organisations, with one being a non-academic entity. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); All applicants are required to consult the detailed guidance documents before preparing their proposal. The application deadline is 23 March 2026 . Application can be accessed from here . To know more about such opportunities, click here . The post Applications for Urban Studies Foundation (USF) Open: Urban Urgencies Research Grants 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Advancing Global Health Program Statement 2026: Funding Opportunity from the U.S. Department of State
summary: The U.S. Department of State s Bureau of Global Health Security and Diplomacy (GHSD) has opened applications for its Advancing Global Health Annual Program Statement (APS) , inviting organizations to submit proposals that support global health cooperation and strengthen international health systems. The funding opportunity operates on a rolling application basis , allowing eligible organizations to submit proposals throughout the year. However, applicants should pay close attention to question submission deadlines tied to review cycles, with upcoming inquiry deadlines including March 27, June 15, August 31, and November 30 . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); This program supports projects that contribute to the implementation of the United States global health strategy and strengthen collaboration with partner countries to address major health challenges. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Supporting Advancing Global Health Cooperation The Advancing Global Health APS is designed to support projects that improve health outcomes through international partnerships, diplomatic engagement, and technical cooperation. The program focuses on strengthening public health systems and supporting countries in becoming more resilient and self-reliant in responding to health threats. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Through this initiative, the Bureau of Global Health Security and Diplomacy aims to operationalize global health strategies by supporting initiatives developed in partnership with national governments. These partnerships are often guided by Memoranda of Understanding (MOUs) between the United States and partner countries, outlining shared goals and commitments for improving health systems and outcomes. Projects funded through the APS are expected to contribute to these strategic frameworks and advance cooperation between governments, international organizations, and civil society actors. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Key Focus Areas The program seeks proposals that advance global health security and strengthen health systems in partner countries. Activities may include: Supporting disease prevention, detection, and response capacity Strengthening national public health infrastructure Improving outbreak preparedness and response mechanisms Promoting health diplomacy and cross-border cooperation Enhancing coordination between governments and global health partners ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Projects should demonstrate how they will support long-term sustainability and build local capacity so partner countries can independently maintain improvements in health systems. Who Can Apply The funding opportunity is open to eligible organizations with experience in global health programming and international development . This includes: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Nonprofit organizations Non-governmental organizations (NGOs) Educational and research institutions Public international organizations Other qualified entities capable of implementing international health programs Applicants are expected to demonstrate strong organizational capacity, experience in managing international partnerships, and the ability to implement programs aligned with strategic global health priorities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Application Requirements Organizations applying to the Advancing Global Health APS must submit a comprehensive proposal package outlining their project concept and implementation strategy. Application materials typically include: A detailed technical proposal describing the project design, objectives, and expected outcomes A budget and budget narrative explaining how funds will be used Organizational background and relevant experience Monitoring and evaluation plans to track progress and impact Any additional documentation required in the program guidelines Proposals should clearly demonstrate how the project aligns with global health diplomacy objectives and supports measurable improvements in public health systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Rolling Deadline and Review Process Unlike many funding opportunities with a single deadline, the Advancing Global Health APS operates on a rolling application basis , meaning proposals can be submitted throughout the year. Applicants are encouraged to plan ahead and submit questions before the designated inquiry deadlines. Current deadlines for submitting questions include: March 27 June 15 August 31 November 30 After submission, proposals undergo a competitive review process in which evaluators assess the project s relevance, feasibility, organizational capacity, and potential impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Strengthening Global Health Security The Advancing Global Health program reflects the growing recognition that health challenges are global in nature and require coordinated international responses. By supporting partnerships between governments, organizations, and health institutions, the initiative aims to strengthen global preparedness for health threats while promoting sustainable improvements in healthcare systems. Through this program, the U.S. Department of State seeks to support innovative projects that enhance health security, foster collaboration, and contribute to stronger and more resilient global health systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Deadline: Applications are accepted on a rolling basis , with question submission deadlines including March 27, June 15, August 31, and November 30 . Application can be accessed from here . To know more about such opportunities, click here . The post Advancing Global Health Program Statement 2026: Funding Opportunity from the U.S. Department of State appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Stephen Lloyd Awards 2026: Funding and Support for Early-Stage Social and Environmental Ideas
summary: The Stephen Lloyd Awards were created to honor the legacy of Stephen Lloyd, a respected leader in the social enterprise sector who believed strongly in the power of innovative ideas to drive social change. The awards aim to identify and support individuals and organizations that are developing new approaches to tackling social and environmental issues. The program is particularly focused on early-stage concepts that need support to move from idea to implementation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Through the awards, selected applicants gain not only funding but also access to experienced mentors and sector experts who can help them refine their ideas, strengthen their strategies, and expand their networks. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); What the Awards Offer The Stephen Lloyd Awards provide several forms of support to selected participants. The goal is to ensure that promising ideas receive the backing they need to grow and achieve real impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Participants can benefit from: Funding to help develop and test their ideas Hands-on guidance and mentorship from experienced professionals in the social impact sector Increased visibility and credibility that can help attract additional partners, supporters, and funding opportunities This combination of financial support and expert guidance makes the program particularly valuable for early-stage innovators. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Who Should Apply The awards are designed for individuals and organizations that are exploring new solutions to social or environmental challenges. Applicants may include founders, charities, and social enterprises that are experimenting with innovative approaches. The program welcomes ideas that are still developing and encourages applicants who may not yet have a fully established project but have a strong concept and commitment to creating positive change. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Successful applicants typically demonstrate creativity, passion for impact, and the potential to develop their ideas into initiatives that can benefit communities and the environment. Why This Opportunity Matters For many early-stage projects, access to the right support network can make a significant difference. Programs like the Stephen Lloyd Awards help bridge the gap between an initial idea and a fully developed solution. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Beyond financial support, the program fosters connections with experienced leaders and practitioners who can help guide innovators through the challenges of launching and growing a social impact initiative. The recognition associated with the awards can also increase the visibility of projects, opening doors to partnerships, collaboration opportunities, and additional funding. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Application Deadline Applications for the Stephen Lloyd Awards 2026 close on April 8, 2026 . Applicants are encouraged to review the requirements and submit their applications before the deadline. How to Apply Interested founders, charities, and social enterprises can learn more about the awards and submit their applications through the official website. To view more awards, Visit OFY here. title: Google Public Policy Fellowship 2026 in the United States (Paid Summer Fellowship – $12,000 Stipend)
summary: Applications are now open for the U.S. Google Public Policy Fellowship Summer 2026 , a prestigious opportunity for students interested in technology policy, internet governance, artificial intelligence regulation, privacy, and digital rights . The fellowship places selected students at leading public interest organizations and policy think tanks in Washington, D.C. , where they will work on real-world policy issues shaping the future of technology. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Fellows will gain hands-on experience in policy research, advocacy, and technology governance , while contributing to national conversations about digital innovation and regulation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Application Deadline: April 9, 2026 (12:00 PM ET) About the Google Public Policy Fellowship The Google Public Policy Fellowship provides students with an opportunity to work on public policy challenges related to the internet and emerging technologies. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Selected fellows will spend the summer working with experts at major policy organizations where they will contribute to research and discussions on issues such as: Artificial intelligence governance and regulation Data privacy and cybersecurity Internet freedom and digital rights Online speech and content moderation Broadband access and digital equity Innovation and competition policy in the technology sector ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Each fellow is assigned a mentor at their host organization and works closely with senior policy staff throughout the fellowship. Fellowship Benefits Participants in the Google Public Policy Fellowship 2026 receive financial support and professional development opportunities, including: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); $12,000 stipend for full-time fellows (40 hours per week) $6,000 stipend for part-time fellows (20 hours per week) Mentorship from leading policy experts and researchers Hands-on experience working on technology policy issues Opportunities to attend policy discussions, government meetings, and conferences Networking opportunities with policymakers, think tanks, and advocacy organizations Payments are coordinated by host organizations and may depend on completion of required documentation and satisfactory performance. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Fellowship Duration The fellowship will run from June to August 2026 . Participants are expected to work either: Full-time: 40 hours per week Part-time: 20 hours per week Most fellows are encouraged to participate in person in Washington, D.C. , although some host organizations may allow remote work. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be at least 18 years old by January 1, 2026 Be enrolled in or accepted into an accredited university or college program Remain enrolled in a degree program through August 30, 2026 Be eligible and authorized to work in the United States Demonstrate interest in technology policy, internet governance, or digital innovation ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Students from all academic disciplines are encouraged to apply. Strong candidates typically demonstrate: Excellent research and writing skills Strong academic performance Experience in extracurricular, professional, or volunteer activities Ability to manage multiple projects in a fast-paced environment Previous Google Public Policy Fellows are not eligible to apply again . Fellowship Responsibilities During the fellowship, participants will support the work of their host organizations through activities such as: Conducting policy research and analysis on technology-related issues Drafting policy reports, white papers, and blog articles Attending government and industry meetings Participating in policy advocacy initiatives Supporting communications and outreach related to technology policy ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); This experience allows fellows to explore career paths in public policy, technology governance, and digital regulation . Host Organizations for Summer 2026 Google Public Policy Fellows will be placed with leading policy institutions and advocacy organizations in Washington, D.C., including: Asian American Justice Center (AAJC) The Cato Institute Center for Democracy and Technology (CDT) Center for European Policy Analysis (CEPA) Chamber of Progress Competitive Enterprise Institute (CEI) Computer Communications Industry Association (CCIA) Connected Commerce Council (3C) Engine Advocacy Foundation for American Innovation (FAI) Information Technology and Innovation Foundation (ITIF) LGBT Tech National Taxpayers Union Foundation (NTUF) NetChoice Open Technology Institute (New America) Progressive Policy Institute (PPI) Public Knowledge R Street Institute Technology Policy Institute (TPI) TechFreedom TechNet ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); These organizations work on critical issues related to technology innovation, internet regulation, digital rights, and artificial intelligence governance . Application Process The fellowship application includes the following sections: Personal Information Current Education References Resume upload Organization preferences Personal statement Final submission ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Important notes for applicants: Reference contact information is required, but reference letters are not mandatory. Host organizations may contact references during the selection process. Applicants may apply to up to two host organizations . Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis , so early submission is recommended. To apply to become a Summer 2026 Google Public Policy Fellow, click here . Key Dates Application Deadline: April 9, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET Fellowship Period: June August 2026 How to Apply Students interested in shaping policy around artificial intelligence, digital rights, internet governance, and technology innovation are encouraged to apply. Applications must be submitted before the deadline through the official fellowship application portal. To apply to become a Summer 2026 Google Public Policy Fellow, click here . Find out more about Host Organizations here FAQ here ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); The post Google Public Policy Fellowship 2026 in the United States (Paid Summer Fellowship – $12,000 Stipend) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: CHLI Global Leaders Program Fall 2026: Paid Public Policy Internship in Washington, D.C. for Students and Recent Graduates
summary: Applications are now open for the Fall 2026 Global Leaders Program, a prestigious semester-long paid internship designed for college students and recent graduates who are interested in gaining practical experience in public policy and leadership. The program is organized by the Congressional Hispanic Leadership Institute (CHLI) and provides participants with hands-on exposure to policymaking by working in both Congressional offices and private sector organizations in Washington, D.C. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The Global Leaders Program is known for offering young professionals a unique opportunity to understand how policy decisions are developed, debated, and implemented across both the public and private sectors. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the CHLI Global Leaders Program The Global Leaders Program is a competitive internship initiative aimed at preparing the next generation of leaders in public policy, international affairs, and governance. Through a structured placement system, interns gain experience in government offices as well as corporate institutions, allowing them to explore how policies affect different sectors of society. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Participants benefit from direct engagement with policymakers, policy professionals, and industry leaders while working in the heart of the U.S. policy environment. This immersive experience enables interns to develop critical policy knowledge, professional networks, and leadership skills that can shape their future careers. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Program Structure and Experience The Fall 2026 Global Leaders Program runs for an entire academic semester and combines professional work experience with exposure to policy discussions and leadership development opportunities. Interns will work in both Congressional offices and corporate environments, gaining a balanced understanding of how public policy interacts with business, governance, and society. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); This dual-placement structure helps participants develop a comprehensive perspective on policymaking, advocacy, and policy implementation. The program also allows interns to strengthen their professional communication, research, and analytical skills through real-world tasks and responsibilities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Program Location and Dates The internship takes place in Washington, D.C., one of the most influential political and policy centers in the world. Program dates: August 24, 2026 December 9, 2026 During this period, interns will live and work in Washington, D.C., gaining firsthand exposure to the U.S. legislative and policy ecosystem. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Who Should Apply The Global Leaders Program is open to motivated students and graduates who are passionate about public policy, leadership, and civic engagement. Ideal applicants include individuals who want to build careers in public service, government, policy analysis, international relations, or advocacy. Candidates should demonstrate strong academic performance, leadership potential, and an interest in contributing to policy discussions and solutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Benefits of the Program Participants in the CHLI Global Leaders Program benefit from several valuable opportunities that can significantly enhance their professional development. These benefits include: Paid internship experience in Washington, D.C. Professional exposure to Congressional and corporate offices Hands-on experience in public policy development Networking opportunities with policymakers and professionals Leadership and career development opportunities The program also helps participants build a strong professional foundation that can support future careers in government, policy research, public affairs, or international relations. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the Fall 2026 Global Leaders Program is April 10, 2026. Interested candidates are encouraged to prepare their application materials early to increase their chances of selection. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); How to Apply Prospective applicants can learn more about the program requirements and submit their application through the official link below. title: Nestlé Needs YOUth Mentorship 2026: Fully Funded Global Mentorship Opportunity for Young Entrepreneurs and Innovators
summary: Young people across the world who are passionate about entrepreneurship, innovation, and career development now have an exceptional opportunity to learn directly from global industry experts. The Nestl Needs YOUth Mentorship 2026 program is part of a broader international initiative designed to empower young professionals, students, and aspiring entrepreneurs with the knowledge, mentorship, and skills they need to succeed in today s competitive economy. Organized by Nestl through its Nestl Youth Entrepreneurship Platform , this mentorship opportunity connects youth with experienced mentors and business leaders who can guide them in building sustainable careers, launching businesses, and developing innovative solutions for global challenges. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the Nestl Needs YOUth Initiative The Nestl Needs YOUth Mentorship program is part of the global Nestl Needs YOUth initiative, a long-running program launched by Nestl to improve youth employability and entrepreneurial opportunities worldwide. The initiative focuses on providing skills development, training programs, internships, mentorship opportunities, and entrepreneurship support for young people under the age of 30. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Through this initiative, Nestl collaborates with industry partners, universities, and entrepreneurship organizations to help young individuals develop future-ready skills and gain access to meaningful economic opportunities. Since its launch, the program has supported millions of young people through training programs, digital events, internships, and mentoring initiatives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The Nestl Youth Entrepreneurship Platform acts as the digital hub where young innovators can access learning resources, join mentorship programs, participate in challenges, and build entrepreneurial skills required for modern careers. What is the Nestl Needs YOUth Mentorship 2026 Program? The Nestl Needs YOUth Mentorship 2026 program is designed to connect young individuals with experienced professionals from Nestl and its partner organizations. Through structured mentorship sessions, participants gain insights into entrepreneurship, leadership, innovation, sustainability, and career growth. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The program typically includes: Virtual mentorship sessions with industry experts Career development guidance Entrepreneurial training and skill-building workshops Networking opportunities with global professionals Access to learning resources and digital courses Exposure to innovation and business strategy in the food and sustainability sectors Participants are mentored by professionals who share real-world insights about building successful careers and startups. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); The mentorship format may include one-on-one sessions, group workshops, and interactive discussions with senior leaders and experts from the corporate and entrepreneurial ecosystem. Who Can Apply? The Nestl Needs YOUth Mentorship Program is designed primarily for young people interested in entrepreneurship, innovation, and professional development. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Eligible participants typically include: University students Recent graduates Early-career professionals Aspiring entrepreneurs Youth innovators and startup founders Individuals interested in sustainable business solutions Many of the Nestl Needs YOUth programs define youth participants as individuals between 18 and 30 years old. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Eligible Countries and Regions The Nestl Needs YOUth Mentorship opportunity is generally open to global participants through the Nestl Youth Entrepreneurship Platform. Eligible applicants may come from: Africa Europe Asia Oceania North America South America Some sessions may focus on specific regions, but the digital nature of the platform allows participation from young people around the world. Qualifications and Requirements Applicants interested in the Nestl Needs YOUth Mentorship 2026 program should typically meet the following criteria: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Age Requirement Applicants are usually expected to be between 18 and 30 years old, as the program focuses on youth empowerment and employability. Educational Background There is no strict requirement for a specific academic discipline, but applicants often come from fields such as: Business and entrepreneurship Marketing and communications Technology and digital innovation Food science and agriculture Sustainability and environmental studies Engineering and data science ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Students and graduates from any academic field with strong entrepreneurial interest are encouraged to apply. Interest in Entrepreneurship and Innovation Applicants should demonstrate interest in: Startup development Business innovation Social entrepreneurship Sustainable food systems Digital transformation Personal career development ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Participation on the Nestl Youth Entrepreneurship Platform Applicants may be required to create an account on the Nestl Youth Entrepreneurship Platform, where they can access courses, resources, and mentorship opportunities. Benefits of the Nestl Needs YOUth Mentorship Program Participants selected for the mentorship program can gain numerous professional and educational benefits. 1. Direct Mentorship From Industry Leaders Participants receive guidance from experienced professionals working at Nestl or within its global partner network. 2. Career Development and Professional Skills The program provides practical insights into career planning, leadership development, and workplace skills. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); 3. Entrepreneurial Skills Training Participants learn how to transform innovative ideas into viable businesses. 4. Networking Opportunities The program connects youth with entrepreneurs, industry leaders, and fellow innovators from around the world. 5. Exposure to Global Innovation Trends Participants gain insight into emerging industries such as: Sustainable agriculture Food technology Digital transformation Environmental sustainability 6. Access to Learning Resources Participants can access online training modules, workshops, and business development tools available on the Nestl Youth Entrepreneurship Platform. Program Focus Areas The Nestl Needs YOUth initiative focuses on three main pillars: 1. Entrepreneurship Supporting young innovators who want to build startups or launch business ideas. 2. Employability Providing training, mentorship, internships, and career opportunities that help young people secure jobs. 3. Agripreneurship Encouraging youth involvement in agriculture and sustainable food production. These areas align with Nestl s broader mission of strengthening the future workforce and supporting economic development among young people globally. How to Apply To apply for the Nestl Needs YOUth Mentorship 2026 program, follow these steps: 1. Visit the official event page 2. Create an account on the Nestl Youth Entrepreneurship Platform. 3. Complete your profile with your personal and professional details. 4. Register for the mentorship opportunity through the event page. 5. Wait for confirmation and further instructions regarding mentorship sessions. Apply here Why You Should Apply The Nestl Needs YOUth Mentorship program offers an invaluable opportunity for young individuals to gain mentorship from global industry leaders, develop entrepreneurial skills, and build professional networks. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); With the global economy rapidly evolving, young people need access to mentorship, training, and career opportunities that help them develop future-ready skills. Programs like this provide the platform and support necessary to transform ideas into impactful businesses and careers. Deadline 10th April 2026 If you are a young entrepreneur, student, or professional seeking guidance and exposure to international industry leaders, the Nestl Needs YOUth Mentorship 2026 program could be a powerful stepping stone toward achieving your goals. title: Study in New Zealand: Lincoln University International Scholarships 2026
summary: Applications are now open for the Lincoln University International Scholarships 2026 , offering financial support to international students who wish to study in New Zealand . The scholarships are offered by Lincoln University to support talented international students pursuing undergraduate, master s, or doctoral studies. The awards aim to help students access high-quality education while reducing tuition costs. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Lincoln University is well known for its expertise in agriculture, environmental management, food science, and sustainability , making it one of the leading specialist land-based universities in New Zealand. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Scholarship Benefits The Lincoln University scholarships provide different levels of financial support depending on the program and academic performance. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Benefits may include: Tuition fee scholarships ranging from NZD $3,000 to NZD $10,000 Merit-based financial support for international students Access to global research networks and academic mentoring Opportunity to study in a top land-based university in New Zealand Some fully funded opportunities are also available through the Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships , which can cover full tuition, living allowance, and additional costs. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); What the Fully Funded Scholarship Covers Under the Manaaki scholarship option, selected students may receive: Full tuition fees Weekly living allowance of about NZ$615 NZ$3,000 establishment allowance Academic and research support networks These scholarships are funded by the New Zealand government to support future global leaders and strengthen development partnerships. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Available Scholarship Types Some of the major scholarships available at Lincoln University include: International Undergraduate Scholarship International Taught Master Merit Scholarship Vice-Chancellor s Scholarship English for Further Study Award Most scholarships are awarded based on academic performance, leadership potential, and personal achievements . Eligibility Criteria To qualify, applicants must: Be international students Meet academic admission requirements Apply for an eligible undergraduate, master s, or PhD program Demonstrate strong academic results or leadership potential ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Some scholarships may also consider country of residence, financial need, or field of study . Study Programs Lincoln University is particularly known for programs in: Agriculture and Agribusiness Environmental Science Climate and Sustainability Tourism and Recreation Management Food Science Business and Finance Application Timeline For many Lincoln University scholarships: Semester 1 deadline: January Semester 2 deadline: June Summer school deadline: October Students applying for government-funded scholarships typically apply between February and March . Applicants are encouraged to prepare their academic transcripts, English proficiency results, and supporting documents before submitting their applications. How to Apply Interested applicants should apply through the official Lincoln University scholarship page. For more Scholarship opportunities, visit OFY here.
